requiredExam.label: Inspect mucous membranes for dehydration
requiredExam.action: Document dry mucous membranes because this changes fluid deficit assessment and monitoring urgency.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect oral mucosa and tongue moisture with attention to dry mucosa, cracked lips, and overall hydration context.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: dehydration, shock physiology, or peripheral perfusion
requiredExam.management_change: Document dry mucous membranes because this changes fluid deficit assessment and monitoring urgency.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Test skin turgor
requiredExam.action: Document poor skin turgor because this supports clinically important volume depletion and fluid replacement urgency.
requiredExam.technique: Gently pinch skin over a clinically appropriate site and observe recoil, interpreting cautiously in older adults or chronic skin changes.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: dehydration, shock physiology, or peripheral perfusion
requiredExam.management_change: Document poor skin turgor because this supports clinically important volume depletion and fluid replacement urgency.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Observe Kussmaul breathing
requiredExam.action: Document deep labored respirations because this changes acidosis severity assessment and urgency of crisis management.
requiredExam.technique: Observe respiratory rate, depth, accessory muscle use, speech tolerance, and breathing pattern at rest.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: respiratory distress, acidosis compensation, pulmonary infection, obstruction, congestion, or pneumothorax pattern
requiredExam.management_change: Document deep labored respirations because this changes acidosis severity assessment and urgency of crisis management.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Test capillary refill
requiredExam.action: Document delayed refill because this changes shock assessment, fluid urgency, and monitored-care level.
requiredExam.technique: Apply brief pressure to a fingertip or toe and record return of color while considering temperature, perfusion, and vascular disease.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: dehydration, shock physiology, or peripheral perfusion
requiredExam.management_change: Document delayed refill because this changes shock assessment, fluid urgency, and monitored-care level.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Palpate peripheral pulses
requiredExam.action: Document weak pulses because this changes perfusion assessment and shock concern.
requiredExam.technique: Palpate bilateral pulses at the named sites, comparing amplitude, symmetry, rhythm, and perfusion.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: dehydration, shock physiology, or peripheral perfusion

requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Palpate distal extremity warmth
requiredExam.action: Document cool extremities because this changes shock assessment and perfusion monitoring.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect and palpate distal extremities for warmth, color, capillary refill, mottling, and symmetry.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: dehydration, shock physiology, or peripheral perfusion
requiredExam.management_change: Document cool extremities because this changes shock assessment and perfusion monitoring.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Palpate abdomen for tenderness
requiredExam.action: Document focal tenderness because this changes concern for pancreatitis, surgical abdomen, infection, or alternate precipitant.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect and palpate the abdomen gently, localizing tenderness and stopping if guarding, rebound, or severe pain occurs.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: abdominal trigger, pancreatitis/surgical mimic, or hyperglycemic-crisis severity
requiredExam.management_change: Document focal tenderness because this changes concern for pancreatitis, surgical abdomen, infection, or alternate precipitant.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

requiredExam.label: Palpate for abdominal guarding
requiredExam.action: Document guarding because this changes surgical mimic concern and escalation pathway.
requiredExam.technique: Inspect and palpate the abdomen gently, localizing tenderness and stopping if guarding, rebound, or severe pain occurs.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

requiredExam.label: Measure blood pressure
requiredExam.action: Document hypotension or severe hypertension because this changes DKA or HHS severity assessment, fluid strategy, and monitoring level.
requiredExam.management_change: Document hypotension or severe hypertension because this changes DKA or HHS severity assessment, fluid strategy, and monitoring level.
requiredExam.limitations: Single readings can be affected by cuff size, positioning, pain, anxiety, arrhythmia, recent activity, and medications; repeat or compare with baseline when results change management.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: hemodynamic safety, hypertension or hypotension severity, and medication or fluid-safety context
requiredExam.technique: Measure blood pressure using an appropriately sized cuff after a brief rest period, seated or supine as clinically appropriate.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

requiredExam.label: Measure heart rate
requiredExam.action: Document tachycardia or arrhythmia because this changes dehydration severity, potassium safety, ECG need, and monitored-care urgency.
requiredExam.management_change: Document tachycardia or arrhythmia because this changes dehydration severity, potassium safety, ECG need, and monitored-care urgency.
requiredExam.limitations: Heart rate is nonspecific and can reflect pain, fever, anxiety, medications, arrhythmia, hypovolemia, anemia, or endocrine state; interpret with rhythm, blood pressure, and context.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: tachycardia, bradycardia, irregular rhythm, or acute physiologic stress
requiredExam.technique: Palpate or monitor heart rate for a full interval, noting rate and regularity.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

requiredExam.label: Measure respiratory rate
requiredExam.action: Document tachypnea because this changes acidosis severity assessment and respiratory support concern.
requiredExam.management_change: Document tachypnea because this changes acidosis severity assessment and respiratory support concern.
requiredExam.limitations: Respiratory rate is often undercounted and may be affected by anxiety, pain, fever, compensation for metabolic disease, or sedation; observe directly when it changes acuity.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: tachypnea, hypoventilation, respiratory compensation, or acute illness severity
requiredExam.technique: Count respiratory rate over a full interval, noting rate and effort.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

requiredExam.label: Measure oxygen saturation
requiredExam.action: Document hypoxemia because it changes respiratory support needs and precipitating infection or cardiopulmonary evaluation.
requiredExam.management_change: Document hypoxemia because it changes respiratory support needs and precipitating infection or cardiopulmonary evaluation.
requiredExam.limitations: Pulse oximetry can be affected by poor perfusion, motion, nail products, dyshemoglobinemia, skin pigmentation, and device quality; interpret with work of breathing and clinical context.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: hypoxemia, oxygen requirement, respiratory support need, or cardiopulmonary acuity
requiredExam.technique: Measure oxygen saturation by pulse oximetry.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

requiredExam.label: Measure temperature
requiredExam.action: Document fever or hypothermia because this changes infection search, sepsis concern, and monitoring level.
requiredExam.management_change: Document fever or hypothermia because this changes infection search, sepsis concern, and monitoring level.
requiredExam.limitations: Temperature depends on measurement site, antipyretics, timing, immune status, and device accuracy; absence of fever does not exclude serious infection or endocrine crisis.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: fever, hypothermia, inflammatory illness, infection, or endocrine-crisis physiology
requiredExam.technique: Measure temperature using an appropriate bedside thermometer route.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

requiredExam.label: Measure current weight if available
requiredExam.action: Document weight because it supports weight-based fluid and insulin planning when local protocol uses weight-based dosing.
requiredExam.management_change: Document weight because it supports weight-based fluid and insulin planning when local protocol uses weight-based dosing.
requiredExam.limitations: Anthropometric values can be distorted by fluid status, clothing, scale differences, height error, pregnancy, body composition, or measurement technique; trend and context matter.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: tachypnea, hypoventilation, respiratory compensation, or acute illness severity
requiredExam.technique: Measure current weight using a calibrated scale when available.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

requiredExam.label: Determine mental status
requiredExam.action: Document confusion, somnolence, or coma because this changes HHS severity, airway concern, and monitored-care level.
requiredExam.technique: Determine mental status using a brief bedside assessment of alertness, orientation, and responsiveness.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: encephalopathy, airway-protection risk, or high-acuity disposition need
requiredExam.management_change: Document confusion, somnolence, or coma because this changes HHS severity, airway concern, and monitored-care level.
requiredExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.

requiredExam.label: Test airway protection
requiredExam.action: Document inability to protect the airway because this changes respiratory support and high-acuity disposition.
requiredExam.technique: Determine airway protection ability by assessing gag/cough reflex and ability to handle secretions.
requiredExam.diagnostic_target: encephalopathy, airway-protection risk, or high-acuity disposition need
requiredExam.management_change: Document inability to protect the airway because this changes respiratory support and high-acuity disposition.
requiredExam.limitations: Do not use provocative unsafe maneuvers; interpret with mental status, respiratory pattern, secretion handling, aspiration risk, local airway protocols, and clinical trajectory.
requiredExam.likelihood_ratio_note: Likelihood ratios are not applicable to routine bedside safety data; use this item to assess acuity, monitoring needs, and management safety rather than diagnostic probability.
requiredExam.when_to_perform: Perform when Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS is the selected validated workup and the maneuver is clinically safe and relevant at bedside.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect jugular venous pressure
conditionalExam.action: Add this when kidney disease, heart failure, or fluid tolerance concern is present because elevated JVP changes fluid safety.
conditionalExam.technique: Position the patient at 30 to 45 degrees when possible and inspect the internal jugular venous pulsation, estimating height and waveform in context.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when heart failure, volume overload, kidney disease, ckd, dialysis, edema or related patient-specific modifiers are present in the selected Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS workup.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: venous congestion, right-heart strain, or volume-sensitive cardiopulmonary disease
conditionalExam.management_change: Add this when kidney disease, heart failure, or fluid tolerance concern is present because elevated JVP changes fluid safety.
conditionalExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect and press peripheral edema
conditionalExam.action: Add this when kidney disease, heart failure, or fluid tolerance concern is present because edema changes fluid safety and monitoring.
conditionalExam.technique: Inspect and press over the lower legs or dependent areas for pitting, symmetry, tenderness, and extent.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when heart failure, volume overload, kidney disease, ckd, dialysis, edema or related patient-specific modifiers are present in the selected Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS workup.

conditionalExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

conditionalExam.label: Auscultate lungs for infection source
conditionalExam.action: Add this when fever, cough, or infection concern is present because lung findings change precipitant workup and antibiotics.

options.label: Skin, wound, line, or dental symptom
Chest pain, stroke, or pancreatitis symptom

conditionalExam.technique: Auscultate symmetric anterior, lateral, and posterior lung fields as feasible, comparing sides for crackles, wheeze, diminished sounds, or asymmetry.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when infection, fever, wound, dysuria, cough or related patient-specific modifiers are present in the selected Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS workup.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: respiratory distress, acidosis compensation, pulmonary infection, obstruction, congestion, or pneumothorax pattern

conditionalExam.limitations: Interpret with clinical context and indicated diagnostic testing for Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS; reliability may be limited by patient cooperation, pain, body habitus, positioning, acute illness, and examiner skill.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

conditionalExam.label: Percuss costovertebral angle tenderness
conditionalExam.action: Add this when dysuria, flank pain, fever, or infection concern is present because CVA tenderness changes pyelonephritis concern.

options.label: Skin, wound, line, or dental symptom
Chest pain, stroke, or pancreatitis symptom

conditionalExam.technique: Percuss or palpate gently over each costovertebral angle and compare sides for reproducible flank pain, stopping if severe pain or instability limits the exam.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when infection, fever, dysuria, flank pain, pyelonephritis or related patient-specific modifiers are present in the selected Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS workup.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: upper urinary tract infection, pyelonephritis, renal colic mimic, or infectious precipitant of hyperglycemic crisis
conditionalExam.management_change: Add this when dysuria, flank pain, fever, or infection concern is present because CVA tenderness changes pyelonephritis concern.
conditionalExam.limitations: CVA tenderness is neither required nor sufficient for pyelonephritis; interpret with fever, urinary symptoms, urinalysis/culture, imaging indications, analgesia response, body habitus, and patient tolerance.
conditionalExam.likelihood_ratio_note: No maneuver-specific LR+/LR- is available in the local validated source metadata; treat this bedside finding as supportive and interpret it with the cited guideline, diagnostic tests, and patient context.

conditionalExam.label: Inspect skin wounds
conditionalExam.action: Add this when fever, wound, ulcer, or infection concern is present because skin infection changes antibiotics and source control.

options.label: Skin, wound, line, or dental symptom
Chest pain, stroke, or pancreatitis symptom

conditionalExam.technique: Inspect the relevant skin, foot, or device site for erythema, warmth, drainage, ulceration, fluctuance, tenderness, or breakdown.
conditionalExam.when_to_perform: Add when infection, fever, wound, ulcer, cellulitis or related patient-specific modifiers are present in the selected Hyperglycemia / possible DKA or HHS workup.
conditionalExam.diagnostic_target: infectious trigger, diabetic complication, or discharge-safety issue
